displayName: Material unavailable (cross-domain ERP↔WMS) — PARKED (guard can never fire)
description: Material unavailable: an active production response requires an article whose free WMS stock is 0.
parkedReason: PARKED 2026-07-12 by CAPT-QTY — this rule HAS NEVER FIRED AND CANNOT FIRE, and was silently pretending to watch. Same root defect as the retired qty_shortfall, mirrored: `when` gates on SMProfile-OperationsResponse.status EQ 'offen', but /api/confirmations carries no status column, so sources/rest/erp-operations-response.json binds that attribute to the CONSTANT 'fertig'. 'fertig' === 'offen' is FALSE for every row that has ever existed ⇒ the precondition is never met ⇒ the reconciler settles 'ok' every single time and the rule can never raise. MEASURED 2026-07-12 against the live engine on .99: 0 open episodes, out of 11.198 multi_source episodes total. This is a SILENT FALSE-NEGATIVE — the worst kind of broken rule, because a dormant watchdog is indistinguishable from a healthy factory. It is parked (visibly dormant) rather than left active (invisibly dormant); ci/lint-cross-constraints.mjs now fails the build on any ACTIVE rule with such a guard, so it cannot be un-parked without fixing it first. TO UN-PARK: the intent ('an ACTIVE order needs material') requires the ORDER's status, which lives on the RIGHT entity — the `when` grammar reaches only the left. Needs `when.rightAttr` support in the it-evaluator, then gate on SMProfile-ProductionOrder.status (measured enum {storniert,abgeschlossen,in_arbeit,freigegeben} — note 'offen' is NOT among them). Also reconsider the left entity: an OperationsResponse is a CONFIRMATION, i.e. production has already happened — checking material availability at that point is the wrong moment regardless of the guard. NOTE: unlike qty_shortfall this rule is genuinely CROSS-DOMAIN (ERP ↔ WMS) and therefore worth repairing — it is real multi-truth, not two views of one source.
$comment: Cross-source facet (CAPT-B Phase 2, building block 3): an active production response (OperationsResponse, status=offen) requires an article whose free WMS stock is 0. The right side is N:1 (many quants per article) → 'aggregate' condenses the free stock (quantity, filtered to blocked==0) via sum. HOLDS (ok) when sum(free) > threshold(0); VIOLATED (raised) at 0. Modeled as a true multi_source class. SAFETY: 0 matches → 'unknown' (no raise before the WMS source has been observed at all). NOTE: needs a next/ quant projection with attributes article_ref/quantity/blocked (article_ref-keyed) — see report open question 'WMS projection'.
